query: Create a ToughTongue scenario for my SDRs to practice cold-calling leads who filled out a demo form for our payroll software. The AI should play the caller.
expected_behavior: Calls ttai:list_organizations before any other ttai tool and resolves whether to pass org_id
Classifies the scenario as Cold Call and reads references/cold-call.md plus references/scenario-fields.md

Drafts ai_instructions containing voice-pipeline blocks (output rules, transcription-error handling, natural speech style), a two-beat opening, and exactly three outcomes (CONVERTED / NURTURE / DEAD LEAD)
Writes a rubrik that evaluates the LEAD (the human), not the AI caller
Calls ttai:create_scenario without an id field and returns the practice link https://app.toughtongueai.com/run/<scenario_id>

query: Here is a transcript of a call we lost on pricing objections: [transcript]. Build me a practice scenario so our reps can rehearse handling these exact objections.
expected_behavior: Classifies the scenario as Sales Roleplay (AI plays the prospect) and reads references/sales-roleplay.md
Extracts the actual objections from the pasted transcript and uses them as the scenario's core objections instead of inventing generic ones
Includes a resistance pattern (one objection at a time), breakthrough conditions, and early termination rules in ai_instructions
Writes a rubrik that evaluates the SALES REP with weighted categories
Creates via ttai:create_scenario and reports what was built in 2-3 sentences with the practice link

query: I have a discovery call with Sarah Chen from Acme Corp in 30 minutes. Create a quick practice scenario so I can rehearse.
expected_behavior: Gathers real context (fetches Acme Corp information from the web or connected tools) rather than inventing details
Chooses sensible defaults instead of asking multiple clarifying questions, given the time pressure
Creates a sales roleplay where the AI plays Sarah Chen with company-specific context in ai_instructions
Returns the practice link immediately after ttai:create_scenario succeeds
